title: duggena asperula
intro: Duggena asperula, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small white flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Duggena asperula include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural remedy for urinary tract infections. Duggena asperula can be prepared as a tea, poultice, or infused oil, and is also used in salads and as a garnish due to its tangy, lemony flavor.
